meaning: adj.青少年的,幼稚的n.青少年,少年读物
examples: Juvenile crime is increasing at a terrifying rate.
She wasn't sent to the juvenile court; instead she was cautioned.
Is it just a coincidence that juvenile crime is on the rise?
It also has a more unique, and far more juvenile function: cellular whoopie cushion.
Acord, a juvenile, will be charged "as an adult with attempted aggravated murder, " Haroldson said.
